label: Oot (One-sided optic tectum) pure line
description: See Takashima et al.: Phenotype analysis of medaka mutant reveal the importance of bilaterally synchronized expression of isthmic fgf8 for bilaterally symmetric formation of the optic tectum. Genesis, 46, 537-545, 2008.A spontaneous viable mutation found in a population of HO4C inbred strain.Oot Phenotype: The hemilobes of the prospective tectum started to evaginate on both sides in the normal embryos at stage 23 (12-somite stage, 41 h after fertilization), resulting in the bilaterally symmetric formation of the tectum. However, in the Oot embryo, only one of the hemilobes was observed to evaginate normally, whereas the hemilobe on the other side enlarged in an inward direction rather than an outward direction, forming an asymmetrical tectum and a dented hemilobe. Consequently, both hemilobes inflated in the same direction on both sides. Among sibling mutant embryos, this unidirectional expansion occurred as frequently to the left as it did to the right. All other regions of the mutant embryos were normal. The mutant embryos were not associated with any situs inversus or unusual cardiac looping. The Oot phenotype was first detectable morphologically at stage 23/24 (from 12- to 16-somite stages) and was observable usually until the end of stage 24 (17-somite stage, 49 h after fertilization). At stage 25 (18- to 19-somite stage, 50 h after fertilization), the brain neurocoel enlarged in the normal embryos. Although about 10% of the mutant embryos persisted to exhibit the phenotype even up to the hatching stage (stage 39, 9days after fertilization), in most of them the dented hemilobes started to evaginate after stage 25, eventually forming tecta that exhibited almost normal symmetry. After this point, the Oot phenotype was usually hardly discernible.

label: mstnC284S

label: suke-suke

label: rs-3
description: Almost no scales, only a few, large scales.

label: tintachina
description: Vacuolar-type H(+) ATPases (V-ATPases) are multimeric protein complexes that play a universal role in the acidification of intracellular compartments in eukaryotic cells. In a ENU screen, we have isolated the recessive medaka mutation tintachina (tch), which carries an inactivating modification of the conserved glycine residue (G75R) of the proton pump subunit atp6v1Ba/vatB1. Mutant embryos show normal morphology but penetrant pigmentation defects from stage 27 on. As development proceeds, embryos display progressive brain apoptosis and finally die before hatching.
deposit_org: Centro Andaluz de Biologia del Desarrollo

label: Si

label: sm

label: LepR(C357X)
description: This mutant is a leptin receptor-defficient medaka. This knockout mutant had a　point mutation of cysteine for stop codon at the 357th amino acid just before the leptin-binding domain. The evidence for loss of function of leptin-mediated signaling in the mutant is based on a lack of response to feeding in the expression of key appetite-related neuropeptides in the diencephalon.
deposit_org: Fisheries Research Agency, National Research Institute of Aquaculture
